  "market_assessment": {

Book is heavily deployed (67%) with 14 holdings, most in green post-Q4 earnings season. Priority is risk management on existing book rather than chasing new highs at extended levels.",

      "nifty_resistance": 24400
    }

      "new_target": 1135.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "+9.3% unrealized winner. SL already at 1064.67 (breakeven+) per existing record. CMP 1083.60 — gap 1.7%, just under 1% trail-gap discipline if I tighten further. Holding SL at 1064.67 (~1.75% below CMP) — within trail-gap rule. Target 1135 still ~4.7% upside. HOLD-equivalent; emitting MODIFY only to confirm levels — actually no-op so switching to HOLD."
    },

      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "PNBHOUSING",

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",

      "timeframe": "SWING",

    },

      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",

    },

      "action": "HOLD",

    },

      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",

    },
    {

      "exchange": "NSE",

    },

    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",

      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.5,

    }

  "watchlist_notes": "BULLISH candidates not admitted with explicit reasons (per Rule 18): KPIL (+0.05) — board meeting May 12-14, 3-5 sessions out, falls inside binary-event window per Rule 11; reject. THERMAX, TATATECH, BHARATFORG (existing OPEN order) — book already 67% deployed, no room within 80% soft target without trim. NH, RAMCOCEM, GABRIEL, LAURUSLABS, FINCABLES, TITAGARH — would push deployment to/past soft cap; current book has 4 pharma/healthcare names (LUPIN, WOCKPHARMA, NAVINFLUOR, MARICO-FMCG-adj), 3 chem (NAVINFLUOR, SRF, PIDILITIND already at cluster cap), 2 capital goods (HAL, GRSE, POLYCAB), 2 financial services (ABCAPITAL, CAMS, PNBHOUSING). Cluster discipline (Rule 10) prevents adding another chem (PIDILITIND/SRF/NAVINFLUOR = 3 names already) or another defense (HAL+GRSE+pending BHARATFORG). SOBHA — re-entry cooldown active from prior exit (Rule 14). Will reassess on Day 20 if HAL exits ahead of May 14 binary or if any current position stops out, freeing capacity.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Book is highly diversified across 14 names at 67% deployed, with 9 of 14 positions in green and combined unrealized +13,707. Today's priority is patience on existing book rather than admit-side activity — Rule 18 zero-admit discipline is satisfied by explicitly walking each watchlist candidate to a reject reason above. Key forward risks: (1) HAL May 14 board meeting binary; consider partial exit Day 20-21. (2) ABCAPITAL sitting on top of SL — Rule 17 says hold, do not panic. (3) Cluster cap effectively reached on chemicals (3 names) and capital goods (2 + pending). Cumulative experiment P&L still slightly negative (-1,893 to +11,817 swing today helping). Win rate 15% over rolling 5d is poor but profit factor 1.1x suggests average win ≈ average loss; need to let winners run further (which trail-gap floor enforces) and avoid panic exits (Rule 17). No new entries today is the disciplined call given book state and binary-event proximity."
